description_long: This bungalow features a low-pitched, multi-sectioned roof with a prominent overhang, typical of suburban residential architecture. The structure is primarily composed of light-colored walls with large windows and wooden framing visible under the eaves. The front facade includes a covered porch area supported by posts, providing a welcoming entrance. The roof is finished in a reddish-brown material, likely metal or tile, and extends over both the main living area and an attached side section. The building sits on a broad, rectangular base that could represent a foundation or patio. The overall design emphasizes horizontal lines and open, airy spaces, with a blend of modern and traditional bungalow elements.
description: A single-story bungalow with a low-pitched, reddish-brown roof, large windows, and a covered porch supported by posts. The structure features light-colored walls and a broad rectangular base, blending modern and traditional elements.
description_view_0: The main facade with the covered porch, large windows, and the prominent overhanging roof is visible.
description_view_1: Side view showing the depth of the roof and the extension of the base, with less detail on windows and porch.
description_view_2: Rear or opposite side view, highlighting the extended base and the back section of the roof, with fewer visible windows.
description_view_3: View from the opposite end of the porch, showing the side elevation and the length of the base.
